initial_program:
% Row and column indices (bounded positively)
row(R) :- input(R, _, _).
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% Exactly one pattern is chosen
1 { p0, p1, p2, p3, p4 : true } 1 :- true.

% Cell facts for each pattern
cell0(0,1). cell0(1,0). cell0(1,1). cell0(1,2). cell0(2,1).
cell1(0,0). cell1(0,1). cell1(0,2). cell1(1,1). cell1(1,2). cell1(2,0). cell1(2,2).
cell2(0,0). cell2(0,1). cell2(1,1). cell2(1,2). cell2(2,0). cell2(2,2).
cell3(0,0). cell3(0,2). cell3(2,0). cell3(2,2).
cell4(0,1). cell4(1,0). cell4(1,2). cell4(2,2).

% Shape cells for each pattern (selected via the true pattern)
shape0(R,C) :- p0, row(R), col(C), cell0(R,C).
shape1(R,C) :- p1, row(R), col(C), cell1(R,C).
shape2(R,C) :- p2, row(R), col(C), cell2(R,C).
shape3(R,C) :- p3, row(R), col(C), cell3(R,C).
shape4(R,C) :- p4, row(R), col(C), cell4(R,C).

% Combined shape predicate (true only for the selected pattern)
shape(R,C) :- shape0(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape1(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape2(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape3(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape4(R,C).

% Guess the majority colour from a shape cell
1 { Col : color(Col) } 1 :- shape(R,C), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% Forbid any other nonzero colour inside the selected shape
:- shape(R,C), input(R,C,X), X != Col.

% Output the colour on the selected shape cells
output(R,C,Col) :- shape(R,C), Col != 0.

% Set all other cells to zero
output(R,C,0) :- row(R), col(C), not shape(R,C).

% Show only the output cells
#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Row and column indices (bounded positively)
row(R) :- input(R, _, _).
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% Exactly one pattern is chosen
1 { p0,p1,p2,p3,p4 : true } 1 :- true.

% Cell facts for each pattern
cell0(0,1). cell0(1,0). cell0(1,1). cell0(1,2). cell0(2,1).
cell1(0,0). cell1(0,1). cell1(0,2). cell1(1,1). cell1(1,2). cell1(2,0). cell1(2,2).
cell2(0,0). cell2(0,1). cell2(1,1). cell2(1,2). cell2(2,0). cell2(2,2).
cell3(0,0). cell3(0,2). cell3(2,0). cell3(2,2).
cell4(0,1). cell4(1,0). cell4(1,2). cell4(2,2).

% Shape cells for each pattern (selected via the true pattern)
shape0(R,C) :- p0, row(R), col(C), cell0(R,C).
shape1(R,C) :- p1, row(R), col(C), cell1(R,C).
shape2(R,C) :- p2, row(R), col(C), cell2(R,C).
shape3(R,C) :- p3, row(R), col(C), cell3(R,C).
shape4(R,C) :- p4, row(R), col(C), cell4(R,C).

% Combined shape predicate (true only for the selected pattern)
shape(R,C) :- shape0(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape1(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape2(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape3(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape4(R,C).

% Guess the majority colour from a shape cell
1 { Col:color(Col) } 1 :- shape(R,C), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% Forbid any other nonzero colour inside the selected shape
:- shape(R,C), input(R,C,X), X != Col.

% Output the colour on the selected shape cells
output(R,C,Col) :- shape(R,C), Col != 0.

% Set all other cells to zero
output(R,C,0) :- row(R), col(C), not shape(R,C).

% Show only the output cells
#show output/3.

cell2(0,0). cell2(0,1). cell2(1,1). cell2(1,2). cell2(2,0). cell2(2,2).
cell3(0,0). cell3(0,2). cell3(2,0). cell3(2,2).
cell4(0,1). cell4(1,0). cell4(1,2). cell4(2,2).

% Shape cells for each pattern (selected via the true pattern)
shape0(R,C) :- p0, row(R), col(C), cell0(R,C).
shape1(R,C) :- p1, row(R), col(C), cell1(R,C).
shape2(R,C) :- p2, row(R), col(C), cell2(R,C).
shape3(R,C) :- p3, row(R), col(C), cell3(R,C).
shape4(R,C) :- p4, row(R), col(C), cell4(R,C).

% Combined shape predicate (true only for the selected pattern)
shape(R,C) :- shape0(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape1(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape2(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape3(R,C).
shape(R,C) :- shape4(R,C).

% Guess the majority colour from a shape cell
1 { Col } 1 :- shape(R,C), input(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% Forbid any other nonzero colour inside the selected shape
:- shape(R,C), input(R,C,X), X != Col.

% Output the colour on the selected shape cells
output(R,C,Col) :- shape(R,C), Col != 0.

% Set all other cells to zero
output(R,C,0) :- row(R), col(C), not shape(R,C).

% Show only the output cells
#show output/3.
